29 - This One Line DESTROYS Price Objections - YouTube.mp4
You see this right here? That's called penny pinching. So when a prospect says, at the end of a conversation, 'Hey, can you do this cheaper?' You need to know exactly what to say. So come over here. I'm going to give you the top two things to actually say to actually overcome this objection, all right? Right when they say, 'Can you give this to me for a cheaper price?' All right? You're going to say, 'Well, I mean, it just depends.' Now, you must have asked me that for a reason. Okay, you never say yes or no. You're just right in the middle. You're indifferent. Well, it depends. Now, you must have asked me that for a reason. Now, typically, they're going to tell you the reason, all right?
Or I might say, you must have asked me that for a reason. What's really going on? Well, yeah, X, Y, Z. company offered it to me for 10% cheaper. Let's say, if they went that route. Then I'm going to say, 'Oh, what specifically did they offer you, though?' Because you have to find out what did the other company—whose price was cheaper— offer them? It could have been something completely different, something that's a little bit different than your product or service. Get the same result. You need to find that detail out. Okay. And let's say they're like, well, I'm not really sure. It was kind of like your thing, blah, blah, blah, blah. Well, if you really thought about it, like what did they actually offer? You. Okay.
You're going to get more industry-specific depending on what you sell. Now, I will tell you when you ask this question, what specifically did they offer you? A lot of times your prospects, if they waffle and like they don't know that could be because they're just lying to you. How many times have you lied to a salesperson, right? You're just negotiating with them and telling them somebody offered it cheaper. They really didn't. So you got to really determine that, based on their tonality, right? That's outside of this reel. Now, let's say they're like, 'Yeah, that company offered it this way.' And they tell me the company. Yeah. I mean, they're fairly decent. I guess it just really depends on, like, the result you want to get.
Now, typically you want to pause right there because a lot of times I'm like, 'Well, what do you mean? Do you mean? Okay.' And then you're going to say, 'So is the cheapest price the most important thing to you or actually getting X, Y, Z result?' And you repeat back the result of whatever you sell that they want is, okay? Let me give you an example of this. I can show you this for every industry. Let's say, if you sold for a marketing agency, right? To a company. So, is the cheapest price the most important thing to you? We're actually getting like higher qualified leads so you can scale the business to $12 million a month. If that's what they said.
They needed higher quality leads to scale to $12 million a month, right? Now, that's a reframe. Now, notice I'm saying, 'the cheapest price.' Hear my tone. Is the cheapest. price the most important thing to you or actually getting X, Y, Z result. Cheapest doesn't sound like that's a good thing, right? Now here, I could go right here. Because, I mean, we could give you a really cheap price. I guess I'm just concerned because how would we be able to dedicate the resources necessary for your internal marketing team? To plug in all that marketing and all that ad creative and all that XYZ to be able to scale you if we don't have the resources from you to be able to do that. It's a how-to question.
Now, I'll give you another example. Let's say, if you sold home improvement windows. So is the cheapest price the most important thing to you or actually putting X, Y, Z? windows. So you still, you stop the cold draft from coming in and you lower your utility bills. See that I'm comparing that cheapest price, or do you want the result you said? Then I could. Come here and say, because we could put in the cheap windows they're offering you. I guess I'd be concerned because how are utility costs going down with those thin windows that they're offering?
